RegnalYear.Monarch: Elizabeth II 芒聙聯 1 Charles III

Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (medical assistance in dying)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (medical assistance in dying)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to provide that a mental disorder is not a grievous and irremediable medical condition for which a person could receive medical assistance in dying.
Whereas Parliament considers it a priority to ensure that adequate supports are in place for the mental health of Canadians;
Whereas Parliament considers that vulnerable Canadians should receive suicide prevention counselling rather than access medical assistance in dying;
Whereas Parliament considers that Canada芒聙聶s medical assistance in dying regime risks normalizing assisted dying as a solution for those suffering from a mental disorder;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Subsection 241.2(2.1) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (2), a mental disorder is not a grievous and irremediable medical condition.
In this section, other Act means An Act to amend the Criminal Code (medical assistance in dying), chapter 2 of the Statutes of Canada, 2021.
If section 1 of this Act comes into force before subsection 1(2.1) of the other Act, then that subsection 1(2.1) is repealed.
If subsection 1(2.1) of the other Act comes into force before section 1 of this Act, then section 241.2 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsection (2), a mental disorder is not a grievous and irremediable medical condition.

If subsection 1(2.1) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 1 of this Act, then that subsection 1(2.1) is deemed to have come into force before that section 1 and subsection (3) applies as a consequence.
